summary: René Descartes reflects on his formal education, the limitations of traditional sciences, and his decision to seek truth within himself and the 'great book of the world'. He establishes that while reason is universally distributed, it must be applied methodically, leading him to the radical decision to dismantle his own received beliefs and rebuild them upon a strictly rational foundation.

principles.statement: Reason is naturally equal in all people; diversity of opinion comes from how we direct our thoughts.
principles.rationale: Everyone believes they have an adequate supply of good sense. The differences in our opinions do not arise because some are more reasonable than others, but because we guide our thoughts along different paths and do not consider the same things.
principles.source_quote: la puissance de bien juger et distinguer le vrai d'avec le faux, qui est proprement ce qu'on nomme le bon sens ou la raison, est naturellement égale en tous les hommes

principles.statement: Having a good mind is insufficient; the primary goal is to apply it correctly.
principles.rationale: Great minds are capable of great vices as well as great virtues. A person walking slowly on the right path will go much further than someone sprinting in the wrong direction.
principles.source_quote: Car ce n'est pas assez d'avoir l'esprit bon, mais le principal est de l'appliquer bien.

principles.statement: To find truth, you must completely dismantle your own received opinions and rebuild them upon the foundation of reason.
principles.rationale: Beliefs accumulated since childhood from various teachers and appetites are inherently flawed and contradictory. True solid knowledge must be built from scratch by a single rational mind.
principles.source_quote: pour toutes les opinions que j'avois reçues jusques alors en ma créance, je ne pouvois mieux faire que d'entreprendre une bonne fois de les en ôter, afin d'y en remettre par après ou d'autres meilleures, ou bien les mêmes lorsque je les aurois ajustées au niveau de la raison.

frameworks.name: Personal Belief Reformation
frameworks.when_to_apply: When seeking a solid, rational foundation for one's own life and knowledge, after realizing the contradictions and uncertainties of received education and societal customs.
steps: Isolate yourself from distractions and passions to reflect deeply.
Make a firm resolution to strip away all previously accepted opinions and beliefs.
Proceed extremely slowly and with great circumspection, like a man walking alone in the dark.
Re-evaluate each belief strictly against the standard of reason.
Adopt new beliefs, or reaffirm the old ones, only after they have been rationally justified.
frameworks.source_quote: je me résolus d'aller si lentement et d'user de tant de circonspection en toutes choses, que si je n'avançois que fort peu, je me garderois bien au moins de tomber.

mental_models.name: The Single Architect
mental_models.description: The concept that systems, buildings, or bodies of knowledge designed by a single rational mind are inherently more orderly, beautiful, and perfect than those patched together over time by many different people.
mental_models.example: Comparing ancient cities that grew haphazardly from small villages over time (resulting in crooked, unequal streets) to well-ordered towns designed from scratch by a single engineer on an open plain.

heuristics: Walk slowly and with circumspection in the dark, so that even if you advance little, you avoid falling.
Do not believe too firmly in anything that has only been persuaded by example and custom.
Look to the 'great book of the world' and practical affairs rather than just the speculations of scholars to find truth.

signature_quotes.text: Le bon sens est la chose du monde la mieux partagée ; car chacun pense en être si bien pourvu, que ceux même qui sont les plus difficiles à contenter en toute autre chose n'ont point coutume d'en désirer plus qu'ils en ont.
signature_quotes.context: Opening line of the Discourse, establishing the universal nature of reason.

signature_quotes.text: Car ce n'est pas assez d'avoir l'esprit bon, mais le principal est de l'appliquer bien.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why having intelligence is not enough without a proper method.

signature_quotes.text: Ceux qui ne marchent que fort lentement peuvent avancer beaucoup davantage, s'ils suivent toujours le droit chemin, que ne font ceux qui courent et qui s'en éloignent.
signature_quotes.context: Emphasizing the importance of method and direction over raw intellectual speed.

signature_quotes.text: Mais, comme un homme qui marche seul, et dans les ténèbres, je me résolus d'aller si lentement et d'user de tant de circonspection en toutes choses, que si je n'avançois que fort peu, je me garderois bien au moins de tomber.
signature_quotes.context: Describing his cautious approach to dismantling his own beliefs.

anti_patterns.description: Attempting to reform a whole state or the public body of sciences from the ground up.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Large societal bodies are too difficult to raise once knocked down, and their fall is devastating. It is better to tolerate their imperfections, which use and time have softened, than to violently overthrow them.

anti_patterns.description: Relying on the plurality of voices (majority consensus) to determine truth.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: For difficult truths, it is much more likely that a single person has discovered them rather than a whole population. Furthermore, what is considered true by consensus is often just a product of local custom and example.

People who overestimate their abilities will doubt received principles and stray from the common path, but because they lack the patience to guide their thoughts in order, they will remain lost their entire lives.
